chinese_summary: 本文研究检索增强流程是否能提升科学论断验证，并比较了 dense retrieval、hybrid retrieval 与 reasoning-augmented verifier。结果显示，检索质量显著限制下游验证效果，而推理阶段能够减少误报，但对证据不完整仍然敏感。
english_abstract_snapshot: The paper compares multiple retrieval strategies for scientific claim verification. Its core argument is that better retrieval is a prerequisite for stronger verification, while reasoning mainly reduces false positives rather than fixing missing evidence.
core_question: How much can retrieval quality and an added reasoning stage improve scientific claim verification on benchmark datasets?
methods: Compare dense retrieval, hybrid retrieval, and a reasoning-augmented verifier on three scientific claim-verification benchmarks.
Measure downstream verification quality and analyze false-positive behavior under different evidence conditions.
key_findings: Hybrid retrieval performs better than dense retrieval across all reported datasets.
The reasoning stage cuts false positives, but gains remain bottlenecked by incomplete evidence retrieval.
Generalization is weaker in low-resource scientific domains.
strengths: Clear ablation between retrieval quality and verification quality.
Findings are tied to concrete benchmark comparisons instead of only qualitative claims.
limitations: The evidence packet suggests weak transfer to low-resource domains.
The reasoning stage cannot fully compensate for missing retrieval coverage.

useful_quotes.quote: Hybrid retrieval outperformed dense retrieval on all three datasets.
useful_quotes.why_it_matters: This is the cleanest summary of the retrieval comparison.

useful_quotes.quote: The verifier reduced false positives by 11.2% relative to the retrieval-only baseline.
useful_quotes.why_it_matters: This quantifies the benefit of adding reasoning after retrieval.

my_connections: Useful reference for building paper-understanding pipelines where retrieval is a hidden bottleneck.
Could be compared with any future Codex workflow that reasons over Zotero annotations plus extracted text.
next_actions: Check whether the paper reports retriever training data composition in more detail.
Compare its hybrid retrieval setup with other literature-note candidates in the same Zotero collection.
